codeReview.summary: The fix adequately addresses all three issues from the prior review: (1) the non-existent `priority` field has been removed from all three occurrences in the SupportTicketCreateRequest and SupportTicketResponse payload documentation; (2) the Section 3.2 RBAC note now correctly states that /api/v1/admin/users IS accessible by SUPER_ADMIN via explicit controller grant, resolving the internal contradiction with the route table; (3) the duplicate numbering in Section 3.2 has been corrected from 1,2,3,3,4,5,6 to 1,2,3,4,5,6,7. All changes verified against source code.

issues.description: The SupportTicketResponse Detail payload lists fields (ticketId, createdBy, assignedTo, comments[]) that don't directly map to the actual SupportTicketResponse record fields (id, publicId, companyCode, userId, requesterEmail, githubIssueNumber, etc.). This appears to be a simplified frontend-facing view of the DTO rather than a verbatim listing, which is reasonable for a handoff document. However, some listed fields (assignedTo, comments[]) do not exist in the DTO at all. This was pre-existing and outside the scope of this fix.

summary: PASS — The fix correctly resolves all three issues from the prior round-2 scrutiny review: (1) The `priority` field has been removed from SupportTicketCreateRequest in all three locations (admin create, dealer create, and detail response). Verified against the actual DTO which only has `category`, `subject`, `description`. (2) The Section 3.2 RBAC note now correctly explains that /api/v1/admin/users IS accessible by SUPER_ADMIN via an explicit controller-level @PreAuthorize grant (confirmed in AdminUserController.java line 20: `@PreAuthorize("hasAuthority('ROLE_ADMIN') or hasAuthority('ROLE_SUPER_ADMIN')")`), resolving the contradiction with the route table. (3) Section 3.2 numbering is now sequential 1–7 instead of the duplicate 3,3,4,5,6 pattern. The lint validation passes. One non-blocking observation: the SupportTicketResponse detail listing includes fields (assignedTo, comments[]) that don't exist in the actual DTO, but this is pre-existing and outside fix scope.
